topic: Someone discovers their neighbor is secretly wealthy
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sound of the imported Italian car idling outside, followed by the faint, expensive scent of jasmine drifting over the fence, set my teeth on edge. I stood by my mailbox, knuckles white where I gripped the metal, my jaw tight enough to crack. For three years, Mr. Henderson had been the quiet, unassuming man who kept to himself, mowing his lawn on a Saturday morning and never speaking unless absolutely necessary. Now, the sheer audacity of his lifestyle—the gleaming new wing of his house, the pristine landscaping that looked like something out of a magazine—felt like a deliberate, cruel mockery of my own cramped, peeling apartment. I watched his chauffeur step out, impeccably dressed, and a hot, prickly feeling rose in my chest. I needed to know what secret funded all that polish.

[story 2]

"Honestly, I don't know what he thinks he is," I muttered to my reflection in the darkened window, scrubbing a hand over my face. The sheer display of effortless affluence next door was grating on my nerves. Every time I walked past, I felt a tightening in my chest, a restless energy that demanded confrontation. I had spent the entire afternoon staring at the wrought-iron gate, trying to find some flaw, some sign that this polished façade wasn't real. The sheer volume of his wealth—the way he treated the neighborhood like his personal estate—made my stomach clench with a sour resentment. I clenched my fists until my nails bit into my palms, wishing I could just shout the question into the expensive silence.

[story 3]

It was the sheer, unearned ease of it that truly set me off. I’d seen the bills—or rather, the lack thereof, the utter absence of visible struggle—and it made my blood boil. I paced the length of my living room, the floorboards protesting beneath my frantic steps. Every glance toward Henderson’s property felt like a physical assault. He never bothered with the small talk, never offered a friendly wave; he simply *existed* in a world clearly separate from mine, a world built on resources I couldn't even begin to fathom. My voice, when I finally called out to my partner, was sharp, strained, laced with an edge of pure aggravation. "Can you believe that guy? He just waltzes around like he owns the place!"

I stood by my mailbox, knuckles white where I gripped the metal, my jaw tight enough to crack. I watched his chauffeur step out, impeccably dressed, and a hot, prickly feeling rose in my chest. I needed to know what secret funded all that polish.

"Honestly, I don't know what he thinks he is," I muttered to my reflection in the darkened window, scrubbing a hand over my face. The sheer display of effortless affluence next door was grating on my nerves. Every time I walked past, I felt a tightening in my chest, a restless energy that demanded confrontation. It was the sheer, unearned ease of it that truly set me off. I’d seen the bills—or rather, the lack thereof, the utter absence of visible struggle—and it made my blood boil. I paced the length of my living room, the floorboards protesting beneath my frantic steps. Every glance toward Henderson’s property felt like a physical assault. He never bothered with the small talk, never offered a friendly wave; he simply *existed* in a world clearly separate from mine, a world built on resources I couldn't even begin to fathom.
